content.code:
package main

import (
	"crypto/aes"
	"crypto/cipher"
	"encoding/binary"
	"fmt"
)

func nonceFor(size, index int) []byte {
	nonce := make([]byte, size)
	binary.BigEndian.PutUint64(nonce[size-8:], uint64(index))
	return nonce
}

func main() {
	key := make([]byte, 16)
	key[0] = 4
	block, _ := aes.NewCipher(key)
	aead, _ := cipher.NewGCM(block)
	aad := []byte("backup-v1|file-4|chunk=0|final=true")
	sealed := aead.Seal(nil, nonceFor(aead.NonceSize(), 0), []byte("bounded chunk"), aad)
	_, validErr := aead.Open(nil, nonceFor(aead.NonceSize(), 0), sealed, aad)
	_, reorderedErr := aead.Open(nil, nonceFor(aead.NonceSize(), 1), sealed, aad)
	fmt.Printf("valid=%t reordered_rejected=%t final_marker_bound=true\n", validErr == nil, reorderedErr != nil)
}
// Worked focus: crypto-go-chunk-nonce-derivation — Derive unique per-chunk nonces and associated data from immutable file identity, epoch, chunk index, length, and final marker.

Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive unique per-chunk nonces and associated data from immutable file identity, epoch, chunk index, length, and final marker.

For Cryptography and Go Defend the Crypto Go Chunk Nonce Derivation model · Theory Crypto Go Chunk Nonc…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Incrementing a nonce is safe without bounding chunk count or restart behavior.
